# Source Card: Neutropenia in Childhood—A Narrative Review and Practical Diagnostic Approach

## Summary
This 2024 peer-reviewed narrative review (Katsaras et al., Hematology Reports, MDPI) establishes the age- and race-specific ANC cutoffs that define pediatric neutropenia, its mild/moderate/severe grading bands, and disease-specific thresholds (cyclic neutropenia periodicity/nadir, Kostmann severe-congenital cutoff). It also proposes a practical serial-CBC diagnostic cadence and a genetics-testing recommendation (ELANE-first). For the pediatric CDS module it is a secondary/derived source: thresholds are cited from upstream references, not newly measured.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Age/race-specific neutropenia definition (<2500 neonates/infants; <1500 toddlers+; 1000-1500 African American children) — locator: Section 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d58b5d6e7b4a805ac0492d8860472938a8ed51b3eb8b450ce51a4905377d5667]"
- (ev_002) Severity bands mild/moderate/severe — locator: Section 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:35f6649a4eae8d13fa0f918447f1fad882c202ba3c951a1ed2c30b64c5766d83]"
- (ev_003) Cyclic neutropenia 21-day periodicity, nadir <200 for 3-5 days — locator: Section 2.1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:11a41e7414baf9f7757185b4399b3ba2d3bca24cc7d94de3aa47264a7aaedde7]"
- (ev_004) Kostmann/SCN: promyelocyte maturation arrest, ANC <500 — locator: Section 2.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:005fde9b9905a8e6c73e1e0fae2d0284549ad19c82d34e6f6b9f86b5d5d8bf42]"
- (ev_005) Serial-CBC diagnostic cadence (2-4 wk repeat; if persistent 2-3x/wk for 6 wk) — locator: Section 5 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8bb95ededc3d341b39a5b2d6d7af27e49fdfba799bc0a2785e625d54ff08b11e]"
- (ev_006) >24 genes, ~60% ELANE, test ELANE in all unexplained congenital cases — locator: Section 5/6 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:864dd284c94a04bd76b4e6f7b51e2d270f32f5b00aed93506c26f2baea6d943e]"
- (ev_007) Infection risk inversely correlated to ANC, worse when marrow-production driven — locator: Section 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9ac2145d7dab435118f28483b7835599888b7b2fa278e5109661dec9a1cc9f6a]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population banding is explicit: neonates+infants carry a higher cutoff (<2500) than toddlers/older children (<1500); a race-based lower limit (1000-1500) is stated for African American children (benign ethnic neutropenia). Sex is not stratified. No gestational-age (GA) or postnatal-age (PNA) neonatal sub-banding is provided beyond "neonates and infants."
- Assay: Section 1 names four analyzer families (Abbott, Siemens, Beckman Coulter, Sysmex) all correlated to the peripheral-smear gold standard; the review presents the cutoffs as method-independent definitions rather than analyzer-specific reference intervals.
- Lifecycle: publication 2024-06; version Hematol Rep 2024;16(2):375-389. The review defers to age-specific institutional reference ranges, or ICSH-published ranges where local ones are absent (Section 5) — so these cutoffs are a fallback, not an override of local intervals.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Fever with severe neutropenia is framed as a medical emergency (abstract + Section 4.5 context) — a red-flag/escalation trigger. Persistent neutropenia beyond the 2-4 week viral window, or ANC <500 with maturation arrest, points to specialist hematology workup (bone marrow aspiration, genetic testing). ELANE testing recommended for unexplained congenital cases. These are referral/scope-exit signals, not executable directives.

## Limitations & conflicts
- UNIT-RECONSTRUCTION FLAG: the PubMed/PMC full-text extraction stripped the "10^9" superscript exponent from every neutrophil count (source rendered as "× 10/L"). All verbatim quotes above reconstruct the standard hematology unit "×10^9/L" (UCUM 10*9/L). The numeric values (2500, 1500, 1000, 999, 500, 200, 2000) are exact as extracted; only the exponent was restored. A locator-faithful re-check against the publisher PDF is advised before any threshold is promoted to an executable CDS rule.
- Secondary source: this is a narrative review; thresholds are cited from upstream references (not independently measured reference-interval studies such as CALIPER). For a definitive pediatric ANC reference interval, corroborate with a primary reference-interval source.
- No neonatal GA/PNA sub-banding: the single "neonates and infants <2500" band does not resolve the well-known early-neonatal physiologic neutrophil surge; do not infer finer neonatal cutoffs from this source.
- Race-based cutoff caveat: the African American lower limit (1000-1500) reflects benign ethnic/familial neutropenia; using race as an implementation variable is a policy decision for the CDS converter, preserved here as reported, not endorsed.
- Extraction dropped some gene identifiers in Section 5 ("associated withgene mutations"); the ELANE attribution is taken from the explicit Section 6 Conclusions sentence, which survived intact.
- No conflicts with other loaded sources recorded at authoring time; conflicts_with is empty.

_Source attribution: According to PubMed, Katsaras G, Koutsi S, Psaroulaki E, Gouni D, Tsitsani P. "Neutropenia in Childhood—A Narrative Review and Practical Diagnostic Approach." Hematol Rep. 2024;16(2):375-389. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.3390/hematolrep16020038); PMID 38921186._
